---
title: "Demo-Bereich"
engine: markdown
---

```{=html}
<div class="page">

<!-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     HERO
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="hero fade-up">
  <div class="hero-eyebrow">Kapitel 4</div>
  <h1>Demo-Bereich</h1>
  <p class="hero-sub">
    Live-Erkennung im Browser, Bibliotheks-Vergleich und eine lokale
    Demo mit OpenCV + YOLO – alles in einem Bereich.
  </p>
</div>

<!-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     LIVE DEMO
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="fade-up fade-up-1">
  <div class="section-label">Interaktiv</div>
  <div class="section-title">Live-Demo: Video-Upload mit Erkennung</div>
  <p class="section-body">
    Lade eine beliebige Videodatei hoch – YOLOv8n erkennt Objekte in jedem Frame in Echtzeit.
    Das Modell läuft vollständig im Browser via ONNX Runtime WebAssembly.
    Passe den Konfidenz-Schwellwert an, um Precision und Recall zu beeinflussen.
  </p>
</div>

<div class="demo-wrap fade-up fade-up-1">
  <div id="drop">
    <span class="drop-icon">🎬</span>
    Videodatei hier ablegen oder klicken zum Auswählen
    <input type="file" id="fi" accept="video/*" style="display:none">
    <span class="drop-hint">MP4 · WebM · MOV · AVI</span>
  </div>

  <div id="bar">
    <span id="st">Modell wird geladen …</span>
    <button id="reload" style="display:none">↩ Neue Datei</button>
    <label>Konfidenz ≥</label>
    <input type="range" id="conf" min="0.10" max="0.90" step="0.05" value="0.30">
    <span class="val" id="confv">0.30</span>
  </div>

  <div id="wrap">
    <video id="vid" loop muted playsinline></video>
    <canvas id="cv"></canvas>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="callout info fade-up">
  <span class="ci">💡</span>
  <div class="ct">
    <strong>Tipp: Konfidenz-Schwellwert</strong>
    Niedrigerer Wert (z.&nbsp;B. 0,15) → mehr Detektionen, höheres Recall, mehr Fehlalarme.
    Höherer Wert (z.&nbsp;B. 0,70) → nur sichere Detektionen, höhere Precision, möglicherweise
    werden schwache Objekte übersehen. Der Standard von 0,30 ist ein guter Ausgangspunkt.
  </div>
</div>

<hr class="divider">

<!-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     BIBLIOTHEKS-VERGLEICH
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="fade-up">
  <div class="section-label">Vergleich</div>
  <div class="section-title">YOLO vs. TensorFlow Object Detection API</div>
  <p class="section-body">
    Neben YOLO ist die <strong>TensorFlow Object Detection API</strong> (TF OD API)
    eines der meistgenutzten Frameworks für Objekterkennung. Beide Ansätze haben
    unterschiedliche Stärken – die Wahl hängt von Anforderungen und Kontext ab.
  </p>
</div>

<table class="cmp-table fade-up">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Merkmal</th>
      <th>YOLOv8n (diese Demo)</th>
      <th>TF Object Detection API</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Architektur</strong></td>
      <td>One-Stage (ankerfrei)</td>
      <td>Two-Stage (Faster R-CNN) oder One-Stage (SSD, EfficientDet)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Framework</strong></td>
      <td>PyTorch (Ultralytics)</td>
      <td>TensorFlow / Keras</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Inferenz-Geschwindigkeit</strong></td>
      <td><span class="badge badge-green">~3–30 ms/Frame</span></td>
      <td><span class="badge badge-amber">~50–500 ms/Frame (Two-Stage)</span></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>mAP auf COCO</strong></td>
      <td>37,3 @ [0.5:0.95] (Nano)</td>
      <td>~28–45 (je nach Modell: SSD bis Faster R-CNN)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Modellgröße</strong></td>
      <td><span class="badge badge-green">~6 MB (nano)</span></td>
      <td><span class="badge badge-amber">~25–200 MB</span></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Browser-fähig</strong></td>
      <td><span class="badge badge-green">Ja (ONNX Runtime Web)</span></td>
      <td><span class="badge badge-amber">Begrenzt (TensorFlow.js)</span></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Training/Finetuning</strong></td>
      <td><span class="badge badge-green">Sehr einfach (1 Zeile)</span></td>
      <td><span class="badge badge-amber">Komplex (Config-Dateien, Pipeline)</span></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Export-Formate</strong></td>
      <td>ONNX, TFLite, CoreML, TensorRT</td>
      <td>SavedModel, TFLite, TF.js</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Community &amp; Docs</strong></td>
      <td><span class="badge badge-green">Sehr aktiv (Ultralytics)</span></td>
      <td><span class="badge badge-blue">Groß (Google-backed)</span></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Lizenz</strong></td>
      <td>AGPL-3.0 (kommerziell: kostenpflichtig)</td>
      <td>Apache 2.0 (frei)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Beste Eignung</strong></td>
      <td>Echtzeit, Edge, Rapid Prototyping</td>
      <td>Forschung, Produktionspipelines, TF-Ökosystem</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

<div class="callout note fade-up">
  <span class="ci">⚖️</span>
  <div class="ct">
    <strong>Wann welche Bibliothek?</strong>
    YOLO ist erste Wahl, wenn Geschwindigkeit und einfaches Deployment wichtig sind.
    Die TF OD API ist besser integriert, wenn ohnehin mit TensorFlow gearbeitet wird
    oder wenn komplexe Two-Stage-Modelle für maximale Genauigkeit nötig sind.
    Für diese Browser-Demo war YOLO mit ONNX-Export die einzig praktikable Option.
  </div>
</div>

<hr class="divider">

<!-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     LOKALE DEMO
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="fade-up">
  <div class="section-label">Lokal ausführen</div>
  <div class="section-title">Lokale Demo mit OpenCV + YOLO</div>
  <p class="section-body">
    Das Skript <code style="font-family:monospace;background:var(--surface-2);padding:2px 6px;border-radius:3px;font-size:13px;">scripts/yolo_demo.py</code>
    führt YOLOv8-Inferenz auf einem Bild oder Video lokal aus und speichert ein
    annotiertes Ergebnisbild. So lässt sich die Demo ohne Browser und mit voller
    GPU-Beschleunigung reproduzieren.
  </p>
</div>

<div class="two-col fade-up">
  <div class="card">
    <h3>📦 Voraussetzungen</h3>
    <p style="margin-bottom:.6rem;">Python 3.8+ und folgende Pakete:</p>
    <div style="background:var(--surface-2);border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:6px;padding:.75rem 1rem;font-family:monospace;font-size:12.5px;line-height:1.8;">
      pip install ultralytics opencv-python
    </div>
    <p style="margin-top:.6rem;font-size:13px;color:var(--text-2);">
      Das Modell <code style="font-family:monospace;font-size:11px;">yolov8n.pt</code> wird beim
      ersten Aufruf automatisch von Ultralytics heruntergeladen (~6 MB).
    </p>
  </div>
  <div class="card">
    <h3>▶️ Ausführung</h3>
    <div style="background:var(--surface-2);border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:6px;padding:.75rem 1rem;font-family:monospace;font-size:12.5px;line-height:1.8;margin-bottom:.6rem;">
      <span style="color:var(--text-3);"># Einzelbild</span><br>
      python scripts/yolo_demo.py --input bild.jpg<br><br>
      <span style="color:var(--text-3);"># Video</span><br>
      python scripts/yolo_demo.py --input video.mp4<br><br>
      <span style="color:var(--text-3);"># Webcam (live)</span><br>
      python scripts/yolo_demo.py --input 0
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="code-block fade-up">
  <div class="code-header">
    <span class="dot" style="background:#ff5f57;"></span>
    <span class="dot" style="background:#febc2e;margin-left:6px;"></span>
    <span class="dot" style="background:#28c840;margin-left:6px;"></span>
    <span style="margin-left:12px;">scripts/yolo_demo.py</span>
  </div>
  <pre><span class="code-comment">"""
Lokale YOLO-Demo: Inferenz auf Bild, Video oder Webcam.
Ergebnisbild/-video wird in runs/detect/ gespeichert.
"""</span>
<span class="code-keyword">import</span> argparse
<span class="code-keyword">import</span> cv2
<span class="code-keyword">from</span> ultralytics <span class="code-keyword">import</span> YOLO

<span class="code-keyword">def</span> <span class="code-func">run</span>(source: str, conf: float = <span class="code-string">0.30</span>, save: bool = <span class="code-keyword">True</span>):
    model = <span class="code-func">YOLO</span>(<span class="code-string">"yolov8n.pt"</span>)

    <span class="code-keyword">if</span> source == <span class="code-string">"0"</span> <span class="code-keyword">or</span> source.isdigit():
        <span class="code-comment"># Webcam: Ergebnisse live anzeigen</span>
        <span class="code-keyword">for</span> result <span class="code-keyword">in</span> model.<span class="code-func">predict</span>(<span class="code-func">int</span>(source), stream=<span class="code-keyword">True</span>, conf=conf):
            cv2.<span class="code-func">imshow</span>(<span class="code-string">"YOLO Demo"</span>, result.<span class="code-func">plot</span>())
            <span class="code-keyword">if</span> cv2.<span class="code-func">waitKey</span>(<span class="code-string">1</span>) == <span class="code-func">ord</span>(<span class="code-string">"q"</span>):
                <span class="code-keyword">break</span>
        cv2.<span class="code-func">destroyAllWindows</span>()
    <span class="code-keyword">else</span>:
        <span class="code-comment"># Bild oder Video: Ergebnisse speichern</span>
        results = model.<span class="code-func">predict</span>(source, conf=conf, save=save)
        <span class="code-func">print</span>(<span class="code-string">f"Gespeichert in: {results[0].save_dir}"</span>)

<span class="code-keyword">if</span> __name__ == <span class="code-string">"__main__"</span>:
    parser = argparse.<span class="code-func">ArgumentParser</span>()
    parser.<span class="code-func">add_argument</span>(<span class="code-string">"--input"</span>, required=<span class="code-keyword">True</span>)
    parser.<span class="code-func">add_argument</span>(<span class="code-string">"--conf"</span>, type=float, default=<span class="code-string">0.30</span>)
    args = parser.<span class="code-func">parse_args</span>()
    <span class="code-func">run</span>(args.input, args.conf)</pre>
</div>

<div class="callout tip fade-up">
  <span class="ci">🖼️</span>
  <div class="ct">
    <strong>Ergebnisbild</strong>
    Nach Ausführung speichert Ultralytics das annotierte Bild automatisch in
    <code style="font-family:monospace;font-size:12px;">runs/detect/predict/</code>.
    Für Videos wird eine annotierte <code style="font-family:monospace;font-size:12px;">.mp4</code>-Datei erzeugt –
    Bounding Boxes, Klassenlabels und Konfidenzwerte werden direkt eingebettet.
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     FOOTER
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="page-footer">
  <div class="pf-info">
    <strong>Demo-Bereich</strong> · Echtzeit-Objekterkennung in Videostreams<br>
    Jasmin Kohlpaintner · Henrik Berg · Markus Luber
  </div>
</div>

</div>

<script>
(function () {

const CLASSES = [
  'person','bicycle','car','motorcycle','airplane','bus','train','truck',
  'boat','traffic light','fire hydrant','stop sign','parking meter','bench',
  'bird','cat','dog','horse','sheep','cow','elephant','bear','zebra','giraffe',
  'backpack','umbrella','handbag','tie','suitcase','frisbee','skis','snowboard',
  'sports ball','kite','baseball bat','baseball glove','skateboard','surfboard',
  'tennis racket','bottle','wine glass','cup','fork','knife','spoon','bowl',
  'banana','apple','sandwich','orange','broccoli','carrot','hot dog','pizza',
  'donut','cake','chair','couch','potted plant','bed','dining table','toilet',
  'tv','laptop','mouse','remote','keyboard','cell phone','microwave','oven',
  'toaster','sink','refrigerator','book','clock','vase','scissors',
  'teddy bear','hair drier','toothbrush',
];

function hsl(name) {
  let h = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < name.length; i++) h = (h * 31 + name.charCodeAt(i)) & 0xffff;
  return `hsl(${h % 360},65%,48%)`;
}

const drop   = document.getElementById('drop');
const fi     = document.getElementById('fi');
const wrap   = document.getElementById('wrap');
const vid    = document.getElementById('vid');
const cv     = document.getElementById('cv');
const ctx    = cv.getContext('2d');
const stEl   = document.getElementById('st');
const confEl = document.getElementById('conf');
const confv  = document.getElementById('confv');
const reload = document.getElementById('reload');

confEl.addEventListener('input', () => confv.textContent = parseFloat(confEl.value).toFixed(2));

const worker = new Worker('worker.js');
let ready = false, sz = 640, provider = 'WASM';
let lastDets = [], running = false;
let resolveResult = null;

worker.onmessage = ({ data }) => {
  if (data.type === 'ready') {
    ready    = true;
    sz       = data.inputSize;
    provider = data.provider;
    stEl.textContent = `✓ Modell geladen · ${provider}`;
  } else if (data.type === 'result') {
    lastDets = data.detections;
    if (resolveResult) { resolveResult(); resolveResult = null; }
  } else if (data.type === 'error') {
    stEl.textContent = `⚠ Fehler: ${data.message}`;
  }
};

drop.addEventListener('click', () => fi.click());
drop.addEventListener('dragover',  e => { e.preventDefault(); drop.classList.add('over'); });
drop.addEventListener('dragleave', () => drop.classList.remove('over'));
drop.addEventListener('drop', e => {
  e.preventDefault(); drop.classList.remove('over');
  if (e.dataTransfer.files[0]) load(e.dataTransfer.files[0]);
});
fi.addEventListener('change', () => { if (fi.files[0]) load(fi.files[0]); });

reload.addEventListener('click', () => {
  running = false; lastDets = [];
  vid.pause(); vid.src = '';
  wrap.style.display = 'none';
  drop.style.display = '';
  reload.style.display = 'none';
  stEl.textContent = `✓ Modell geladen · ${provider}`;
});

function load(file) {
  vid.src = URL.createObjectURL(file);
  vid.onloadeddata = () => {
    drop.style.display = 'none';
    reload.style.display = '';
    wrap.style.display  = 'block';
    vid.play();
    if (!running) { running = true; inferLoop(); drawLoop(); }
  };
}

const off  = document.createElement('canvas');
const octx = off.getContext('2d', { willReadFrequently: true });

function prep(src, W, H) {
  const scale = Math.min(sz / W, sz / H);
  const nw = Math.round(W * scale), nh = Math.round(H * scale);
  const px = Math.floor((sz - nw) / 2), py = Math.floor((sz - nh) / 2);
  off.width = sz; off.height = sz;
  octx.fillStyle = 'rgb(114,114,114)';
  octx.fillRect(0, 0, sz, sz);
  octx.drawImage(src, px, py, nw, nh);
  const pix = octx.getImageData(0, 0, sz, sz).data;
  const f32 = new Float32Array(3 * sz * sz);
  for (let i = 0; i < sz * sz; i++) {
    f32[i]               = pix[i * 4]     / 255;
    f32[sz * sz + i]     = pix[i * 4 + 1] / 255;
    f32[2 * sz * sz + i] = pix[i * 4 + 2] / 255;
  }
  return { f32, scale, padX: px, padY: py };
}

async function inferLoop() {
  while (running) {
    if (!ready || vid.readyState < 2 || vid.paused) {
      await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50)); continue;
    }
    const W = vid.videoWidth, H = vid.videoHeight;
    if (!W) { await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 16)); continue; }
    const t0 = performance.now();
    const { f32, scale, padX, padY } = prep(vid, W, H);
    await new Promise(r => {
      resolveResult = r;
      worker.postMessage({
        type: 'infer', float32: f32,
        scale, padX, padY, origW: W, origH: H,
        confThresh: parseFloat(confEl.value),
      }, [f32.buffer]);
    });
    const fps = (1000 / (performance.now() - t0)).toFixed(1);
    stEl.textContent = `${provider} · ${fps} FPS · ${lastDets.length} Objekt(e) erkannt`;
  }
}

function drawLoop() {
  if (!running) return;
  const W = vid.videoWidth, H = vid.videoHeight;
  if (W > 0 && H > 0) {
    if (cv.width !== W || cv.height !== H) { cv.width = W; cv.height = H; }
    ctx.clearRect(0, 0, W, H);
    for (const d of lastDets) {
      const c = hsl(d.class);
      const [x, y, w, h] = d.bbox;
      ctx.strokeStyle = c; ctx.lineWidth = 2.5;
      ctx.strokeRect(x, y, w, h);
      const lbl = `${d.class} ${Math.round(d.score * 100)}%`;
      ctx.font = 'bold 24px DM Sans, system-ui, sans-serif';
      const tw = ctx.measureText(lbl).width;
      ctx.fillStyle = c;
      ctx.fillRect(x, y - 30, tw + 12, 30);
      ctx.fillStyle = '#fff';
      ctx.fillText(lbl, x + 6, y - 7);
    }
  }
  requestAnimationFrame(drawLoop);
}

})();
</script>
```
